- **Why Embodied Carbon?** Because *operational* carbon (from energy used in buildings) is decreasing due to solar/wind, while *embodied* carbon is now the *dominant* share of a structure's lifetime footprint.
- **Why Toxicity?** Lead paint, asbestos, CCA-treated timber, and PVC release carcinogens and endocrine disruptors across decades.
- **Why Local Availability?** Transport by road adds roughly **0.0007 kg CO₂/km/kg** of cargo — small per kg, but huge cumulatively.

## 2.3 KTU Formula Sheet / Cheat Sheet

| # | Formula / Parameter | Description | Units |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | $EE_{total} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} m_i \cdot EE_i$ | Total embodied energy = mass × unit EE, summed over materials | GJ or MJ |
| 2 | $EC_{total} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} m_i \cdot EC_i$ | Total embodied carbon (kg CO₂-eq) | kg CO₂-eq |
| 3 | $TES = w_1 \cdot EE_n + w_2 \cdot EC_n + w_3 \cdot T_n + w_4 \cdot L_n$ | Total Environmental Score (weighted sum, $w_j$ = weight, all normalized) | Dimensionless |
| 4 | $CF = \dfrac{EC_{total}}{Service\ Life \times Area}$ | Carbon Footprint per m² per year | kg CO₂-eq/m²·yr |
| 5 | $R = \dfrac{m_{recycled}}{m_{total}} \times 100\%$ | Recycled content ratio | % |
| 6 | $\eta_{recovery} = \dfrac{m_{recovered}}{m_{input}} \times 100\%$ | End-of-life recovery efficiency | % |
| 7 | $LCA_{score} = \int_{cradle}^{grave} f(t) \cdot dt$ | Cradle-to-grave impact integral over life cycle | Various |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Normalization Rule (KTU pattern):** When using $TES$, every indicator $EE_n, EC_n, T_n, L_n$ must be **normalized to a 0–1 scale** by dividing by the worst-case value in the candidate set.

## 2.4 Real-World Utility

This selection framework is used by:
- **Green building rating systems**: LEED, GRIHA, BREEAM (embodied carbon credits).
- **Public infrastructure projects**: EU's *Level(s)* framework for sustainable buildings.
- **Ecological restoration**: bio-engineering structures like live cribwalls, vegetated gabions, and rammed earth where local soil replaces concrete.

<!-- SECTION_2_END -->

<!-- SECTION_3_START -->
# 📘 SECTION 3: Step-by-Step Derivations & Analytical Matrix

## 3.1 Worked Example: Comparing Three Wall Materials

> [!NOTE]
> **KTU-style Problem:** A designer must select a wall material for a **100 m², single-storey eco-lodge** with a **design service life of 50 years**. Three options are shortlisted: (A) Burnt Clay Brick, (B) Stabilized Compressed Earth Block (SCEB), (C) Autoclaved Aerated Concrete (AAC). Use the data below and apply the *5-step hierarchical selection* to recommend the optimum material.

| Material | Unit EE (GJ/t) | Unit EC (kg CO₂/t) | Strength (MPa) | Local? | Recyclable? | Toxicity Score (0–100, lower=better) | Required Mass for 100 m² wall (t) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A. Burnt Brick | 4.5 | 240 | 10 | Yes | Partial | 35 | 18 |
| B. SCEB | 0.8 | 22 | 4 | Yes | Yes | 5 | 22 |
| C. AAC Block | 3.5 | 300 | 4 | No | Partial | 20 | 9 |

### Step 1 — Functional Filter

- Required strength for a single-storey wall: ~3–4 MPa.
- **Burnt Brick (10 MPa)**, **SCEB (4 MPa)**, **AAC (4 MPa)** — **all pass**.

### Step 2 — Compute $EE_{total}$ and $EC_{total}$

Using $EE_{total} = \sum m_i \cdot EE_i$:

$$EE_A = 18 \times 4.5 = 81.0 \text{ GJ}$$

$$EE_B = 22 \times 0.8 = 17.6 \text{ GJ}$$

$$EE_C = 9 \times 3.5 = 31.5 \text{ GJ}$$

Using $EC_{total} = \sum m_i \cdot EC_i$:

$$EC_A = 18 \times 240 = 4320 \text{ kg CO}_2\text{-eq}$$

$$EC_B = 22 \times 22 = 484 \text{ kg CO}_2\text{-eq}$$

$$EC_C = 9 \times 300 = 2700 \text{ kg CO}_2\text{-eq}$$

### Step 3 — Compute Annualized Carbon Footprint (CF)

Using $CF = \dfrac{EC_{total}}{Service\ Life \times Area}$:

$$CF_A = \frac{4320}{50 \times 100} = 0.864 \text{ kg CO}_2\text{-eq/m}^2\text{·yr}$$

$$CF_B = \frac{484}{50 \times 100} = 0.0968 \text{ kg CO}_2\text{-eq/m}^2\text{·yr}$$

$$CF_C = \frac{2700}{50 \times 100} = 0.540 \text{ kg CO}_2\text{-eq/m}^2\text{·yr}$$

### Step 4 — Toxicity & Local Filter

- Brick: Toxicity = 35 (high kiln emissions of SO₂, fluorides).
- SCEB: Toxicity = 5 (only ~5–10% cement stabilizer; non-toxic).
- AAC: Toxicity = 20 (aluminium powder + lime; not local).

> [!IMPORTANT]
> SCEB passes all five pillars. AAC fails *Local Availability*. Brick fails the *Toxicity + Energy* tests. **SCEB is the ecological optimum.**

### Step 5 — Verification (TES Calculation)

Assign equal weights $w_1 = w_2 = w_3 = w_4 = 0.25$. Normalize each indicator by the worst observed value:

$$EE_n(A) = \frac{81}{81} = 1.00,\ \ EE_n(B) = \frac{17.6}{81} = 0.217,\ \ EE_n(C) = \frac{31.5}{81} = 0.389$$

$$EC_n(A) = \frac{4320}{4320} = 1.00,\ \ EC_n(B) = \frac{484}{4320} = 0.112,\ \ EC_n(C) = \frac{2700}{4320} = 0.625$$

$$T_n(A) = \frac{35}{35} = 1.00,\ \ T_n(B) = \frac{5}{35} = 0.143,\ \ T_n(C) = \frac{20}{35} = 0.571$$

$$L_n(A) = 0.5\ (\text{partial}),\ \ L_n(B) = 0.0\ (\text{fully local}),\ \ L_n(C) = 1.0\ (\text{imported})$$

$$TES_A = 0.25(1) + 0.25(1) + 0.25(1) + 0.25(0.5) = 0.875$$

$$TES_B = 0.25(0.217) + 0.25(0.112) + 0.25(0.143) + 0.25(0.0) = 0.118$$

$$TES_C = 0.25(0.389) + 0.25(0.625) + 0.25(0.571) + 0.25(1.0) = 0.646$$

> [!NOTE]
> **Result:** $TES_B \ (0.118) \ll TES_C \ (0.646) \ll TES_A \ (0.875)$. The **Stabilized Compressed Earth Block (SCEB) wins by a factor of ~5.5 over burnt brick and ~5.5 over AAC**, confirming the hierarchical result.

## 3.2 Analytical Comparison Matrix (Summary)

| Indicator | Burnt Brick (A) | SCEB (B) | AAC (C) | Winner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Functional strength | 10 MPa | 4 MPa | 4 MPa | A (marginal) |
| $EE_{total}$ | 81.0 GJ | 17.6 GJ | 31.5 GJ | **B** |
| $EC_{total}$ | 4320 kg | 484 kg | 2700 kg | **B** |
| Toxicity | 35 | 5 | 20 | **B** |
| Local | Yes | Yes | No | A / B |
| TES (lower=better) | 0.875 | 0.118 | 0.646 | **B** |

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
# 📘 SECTION 4: Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Material Selection Decision Flow (Block-Level Functional Architecture)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    startA[Start: Design Requirement Identified] --> s1
    s1["step1: Functional Filter"] --> s1q{Strength and Durability Met?}
    s1q -- No --> rej1[Reject Material]
    s1q -- Yes --> s2
    s2["step2: Energy Filter EE Total"] --> s2q{EE less than 30 GJ per tonne?}
    s2q -- No --> s2sub[Flag for Substitution]
    s2q -- Yes --> s3
    s3["step3: Toxicity Filter Eco 99"] --> s3q{Toxicity less than 20?}
    s3q -- No --> rej2[Reject Material]
    s3q -- Yes --> s4
    s4["step4: Local Filter Transport"] --> s4q{Sourced within 100 km?}
    s4q -- No --> pen1[Penalize Score]
    s4q -- Yes --> s5
    s5["step5: End of Life Filter C2C Index"] --> s5q{C2C Index at least 50?}
    s5q -- No --> rej3[Reject Material]
    s5q -- Yes --> opt
    opt["opt: Optimize via TES Minimum"] --> outA[Selected Ecological Material]
    s2sub --> s1
```

## 4.2 Material Life Cycle Phases (Cradle-to-Cradle Block Schematic)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    cra["cra: CRADLE Raw Extraction"] --> man["man: MANUFACTURE"]
    man --> tra["tra: TRANSPORT"]
    tra --> use["use: USE PHASE 50 Years"]
    use --> eol["eol: END OF LIFE"]
    eol --> br1["br1: BIOLOGICAL CYCLE Compostable Soil"]
    eol --> br2["br2: TECHNICAL CYCLE Reuse Recycle"]
    br1 --> nat["nat: RETURN TO NATURE"]
    br2 --> cra
```

## 4.3 Indicator Hierarchy (Weighted Pyramid)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    top["top: TOTAL ENVIRONMENTAL SCORE TES"]
    top --> p1["p1: Embodied Energy Weight 0.25"]
    top --> p2["p2: Embodied Carbon Weight 0.25"]
    top --> p3["p3: Eco Toxicity Weight 0.25"]
    top --> p4["p4: Local Sourcing Weight 0.25"]
    p1 --> n1["n1: Normalized to 0 1 scale"]
    p2 --> n1
    p3 --> n1
    p4 --> n1
    n1 --> rank["rank: RANK CANDIDATES MIN TES WINS"]
```
